# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 136

This is turn 136. Turns 1 through 135 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 136.

## Turn 136 — the quartic residual is a genuine \(C_4\)-framed invariant

Turn 135 showed that the obsolete and repaired marked pro-\(2\) relators agree through the third Zassenhaus quotient after the Nielsen shear
\[
x_1\longmapsto x_1[x_0,\sigma],
\]
and isolated their first residual term in degree four.  The repaired \(h_0\)-word had already passed the complete first- and second-order coefficient gate. 

Write
\[
r_{\rm old}=x_0^2x_1^3x_1^\sigma,\qquad
r_{\rm new}=x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma],
\]
and use Magnus variables
\[
\sigma\mapsto1+A,\qquad x_0\mapsto1+B,\qquad x_1\mapsto1+C.
\]
Their common quadratic term is
\[
q_2=B^2+AC+CA.
\]

### The complete framed IA orbit

I fixed the unramified \(C_4\)-frame exactly. Thus the permitted degree-two corrections to each generator lie in
\[
K_2=\left\langle
B^2,\ C^2,\ [B,A],\ [C,A],\ [C,B]
\right\rangle.
\]
The omitted sixth degree-two basis vector \(A^2\) changes the Frobenius frame modulo \(4\), for example \(\sigma\mapsto\sigma^3\), and is therefore forbidden. The result below is intentionally a framed invariant, not an unmarked invariant.

There are \(15\) degree-two IA parameters. Matching the cubic jet gives a rank-\(8\) affine system, hence \(2^7=128\) solutions. One equation is decisive:

\[
\text{coefficient of }[B,A]\text{ in the correction to }C=1.
\tag{136.1}
\]

Now define the quartic functional
\[
\kappa(f)=[ABAB]\,f,
\]
the coefficient of the monomial \(ABAB\).

For every one of the \(128\) cubic-matching degree-two IA jets,
\[
\kappa\!\left(\operatorname{in}_4\bigl(\phi(r_{\rm old})r_{\rm new}^{-1}\bigr)\right)=1.
\tag{136.2}
\]
In fact, this coefficient is exactly the forced bit in (136.1).

Arbitrary degree-three corrections to the three generators contribute an \(18\)-dimensional quartic subspace, and \(\kappa\) vanishes on that entire subspace. Changes of normal relator generator contribute the degree-four restricted-Lie ideal
\[
I_4=
\left\langle
q_2^{[2]},\ [q_2,L_2],\ [[q_2,L_1],L_1]
\right\rangle ,
\]
which has dimension \(9\); again,
\[
\kappa(I_4)=0.
\tag{136.3}
\]

The full mod-\(2\) linear stabilizer of the exact unramified character and \(q_2\) has only two elements:
\[
1,\qquad A\longmapsto A+C,\quad B\longmapsto B,\quad C\longmapsto C.
\]
I checked both lifts, and (136.2) persists.

Therefore
\[
\boxed{\text{No exact-\(C_4\)-framed IA transformation through degree three
carries }r_{\rm old}\text{ to }r_{\rm new}\pmod{D_5}.}
\]

This remains true after allowing inversion, conjugation, or replacement of the relator by another normal generator. The degree-four residual found in Turn 135 is thus genuine in the framed category.

### The fourth-jet quotient

Set
\[
J_4(r)=F/\overline{\langle D_5F,r\rangle},
\]
where \(F\) is free pro-\(2\) on \(\sigma,x_0,x_1\).

Using the standard mild one-relator calculation for the nonsingular initial form \(q_2\), the free restricted-Lie dimensions through degree four are
\[
3,\ 6,\ 8,\ 24,
\]
while the relation ideal removes
\[
0,\ 1,\ 3,\ 9
\]
dimensions. Hence
\[
|J_4(r_{\rm old})|
=
|J_4(r_{\rm new})|
=
2^{3+5+5+15}
=
2^{28}.
\]
They have the same \(2^{13}\)-element third jet, but they are not isomorphic as exact-\(C_4\)-framed fourth jets.

The computation is reproducible here:

[Turn 136 quartic-orbit certificate](sandbox:/mnt/data/turn136_quartic_orbit_certificate.py)

### One uniform family replacing the isolated tame tests

Let
\[
T_{e,m}=C_e\rtimes C_{2^m},\qquad
t^s=t^2,\qquad \operatorname{ord}_e(2)=2^m,\qquad m\ge2.
\]
This includes \(e=5,m=2\) and \(e=17,m=3\).

The tame frame is rigid. If
\[
t'=t^v,\qquad s'=t^a s^u
\]
is any generating tame pair satisfying \((t')^{s'}=(t')^2\), then
\[
2^u\equiv2\pmod e,
\]
so
\[
u\equiv1\pmod{2^m}.
\]
Thus the tame relation itself fixes the entire unramified \(C_{2^m}\)-frame; the forbidden \(A^2\)-freedom cannot occur.

Put \(k=\min(m,3)\) and define
\[
J_{4,m}(r)
=
J_4(r)\times_{C_{2^k}}C_{2^m},
\]
followed by the uniform pullback
\[
G_{e,m}(r)
=
T_{e,m}\times_{C_{2^m}}J_{4,m}(r).
\tag{136.4}
\]

For \(r=r_{\rm new}\):

- The true local group surjects onto \(G_{e,m}(r_{\rm new})\): combine its tame \(T_{e,m}\)-quotient with its corrected marked maximal pro-\(2\) fourth jet. The two kernels have coprime odd and \(2\)-power orders, so the resulting subdirect product is the full fiber product.
- The repaired \(h_0\)-candidate has a canonical surjection. In the \(2\)-group factor, \(\tau=1\), hence \(u_i=x_i\), \(d_0=c_0=1\), and its wild relation reduces exactly to
  \[
  x_0^{\sigma^2}x_0[x_1,\sigma]=1.
  \]
- The retired \(A_2\)-word has no framed surjection onto this target. Any such surjection would induce an exact-\(C_{2^m}\)-framed epimorphism
  \[
  J_{4,m}(r_{\rm old})\twoheadrightarrow J_{4,m}(r_{\rm new}),
  \]
  contradicting the quartic invariant.

Thus (136.4) is a single infinite local-quotient family that the \(h_0\)-repair passes and the obsolete word fails. It simultaneously replaces the proposed \(C_5\rtimes C_4\) and \(C_{17}\rtimes C_8\) case checks.

**ESTABLISHED**: Computed the full exact-\(C_4\)-framed IA orbit through degree three. The quartic functional \(\kappa=[ABAB]\) equals \(1\) on every cubic-matching residual and vanishes on all degree-three corrections and normal-relator ambiguity. Hence the Turn-135 quartic residual is a genuine framed invariant. Constructed the uniform local-quotient family \(G_{e,m}(r_{\rm new})\), on which the repaired \(h_0\)-word has a canonical surjection while the retired \(A_2\)-word has none.

**GAP**: The \(h_0\)-word’s complete surjection count on these fourth-jet targets is not yet known, and the private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. Passing this necessary fourth-order gate does not yet prove the presentation.

**NEXT**: Compress the \(15\)-dimensional fourth layer to the smallest exact-\(C_4\)-framed characteristic central quotient carrying the stabilizer orbit of \(\kappa\). This should reduce the \(2^{28}\) jet to a pro-\(2\) target of order at most \(2^{15}\), while retaining the uniform separation. Then compute exact framed counts on the resulting \(T_{e,m}\)-pullback family before advancing to degree five.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
